seoTitle: Gregory Hills vs Grenfell — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gregory Hills and Grenfell. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Gregory Hills vs Grenfell: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gregory Hills and Grenfell.
heading: Gregory Hills vs Grenfell — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gregory Hills, New South Wales and Grenfell, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Grenfell has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Gregory Hills (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gregory Hills or Grenfell safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gregory Hills has a flood risk score of 20/100, while Grenfell scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gregory Hills scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Grenfell scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gregory Hills or Grenfell?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Grenfell has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
